---
title: "Longitudinal growth comparison"
format: html
---


```{r}
library(tidyverse)
#devtools::install_git(url="https://github.com/levante-framework/rlevante")
library(rlevante) 
library(viridis)
```


```{r}
dataset_spec <- list(list(name = "pilot_uniandes_co_rural_processed:bxgv", 
                          version = "current"),
                     list(name = "pilot_uniandes_co_bogota_processed:d0c5", 
                          version = "current"),
                     list(name = "pilot_mpieva_de_main_processed:8wjx", 
                          version = "current"), 
                     list(name = "pilot_western_ca_main_processed:bgcj", 
                          version = "current")) 

scores_list <- map(dataset_spec, \(d) rlevante::get_scores(d$name))

scores <- scores_list |>
  bind_rows()

```

Data cleanup

```{r}
scores_cleaned <- scores |>
  filter(age > 5, age < 13, 
         score > -6, score < 6) |>
  group_by(dataset, task_id) |>
  mutate(std_score = (score - mean(score, na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(score, na.rm = TRUE))
```


Retest examination

```{r}
retest_scores <- scores_cleaned |> 
  group_by(dataset, user_id, task_id) |>
  arrange(user_id, task_id, age) |>
  filter(n() > 1) |>
  mutate(run_number = row_number(),
         age_gap = c(0, diff(age))) 
```



```{r}
ggplot(filter(retest_scores, task_id != "pa"), 
       aes(x = age, y = score, col = dataset)) + 
  geom_line(aes(group = user_id), alpha = .5) + 
  facet_wrap(~task_id, scales = "free") + 
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.direction = "vertical")
```

Memory, vocab, matrix in german longitudinal data have constant offsets. 


```{r}
bad_long <- filter(retest_scores, 
              task_id %in% c("memory-game","vocab","matrix-reasoning"), 
              dataset == "pilot_mpieva_de_main")

ggplot(bad_long, 
       aes(x = age, y = score, col = dataset)) + 
  geom_line(aes(group = user_id), alpha = .5) + 
  facet_wrap(~task_id, scales = "free") + 
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.direction = "vertical")
```
Look at difference scores. 

```{r}
bad_wide <- bad_long |>
  filter(run_number < 3) |>
  group_by(user_id, task_id) |>
  mutate(first_age = min(age), 
         any_adaptive = any(adaptive)) |>
  select(dataset, task_id, score, run_number, first_age, any_adaptive) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = "run_number", 
              values_from = "score") |>
  mutate(score_diff = `2`-`1`)

ggplot(bad_wide, aes(x = score_diff)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = .1) + 
  facet_wrap(~task_id)
  
```

```{r}
View(bad_wide)
```

Zoom in on trials. 

```{r}
de_trials <- rlevante::get_trials(data_source = "pilot_mpieva_de_main_processed:8wjx", 
                                  version = "current")

de_vocab_long <- filter(de_trials, task_id %in% c("vocab")) |>
  filter(user_id %in% filter(bad_wide, task_id=="vocab")$user_id)

filter(de_vocab_long, user_id == "Lva3b3JvCLBS4MsOklmB") |> 
  arrange(run_id, trial_number) |> View()
```

Working hypothesis is that the CAT vocab allowed german kids to get to the harder words, but in the non-cat version they were at ceiling so much that they got an extremely high score. They timed out before seeing any hard words. 

```{r}
bad_long |>
  group_by(task_id, run_number, adaptive) |>
  count()
```
